good_examples._comment: Correct: complete sentences, verbatim evidence, proper decision fields, valid edges with from_content/to_content
good_examples.input_chunk:
After extensive backtesting across 18 months of data, we decided to use day-level bars for V8 instead of the 15-minute intraday bars used in V7. The primary reason is that bar-level noise in the 15-min timeframe introduced too many false signals in the momentum indicator, leading to a 40% increase in false-positive trade entries.

The lookback window is set to 60 trading days, consistent with V7.

Risk: switching to daily bars reduces our ability to react to intraday volatility spikes. This is partially mitigated by the overnight position limit of $500K.

bad_examples._comment: Wrong: content is a fragment, no evidence_quote, manually assigned ID
bad_examples.input_chunk: After extensive backtesting, we decided to use day-level bars instead of intraday bars for V8.
problems: content is a fragment ('day-level bars'), not a self-contained sentence
evidence_quote is empty — must be verbatim from chunk
missing decision-specific fields (title, context, rationale, scope, status_hint)

bad_examples._comment: Wrong: extracting a heading as a node, creating edge with wrong field names

The model uses a stop-loss of 2% per trade to limit downside.
